Import and prepare swe eel data

Author

Viktor Thunell

Published

June 5, 2026

Introduction

Load libraries

pkgs <- c("tidyverse", "devtools","tidylog","stacomiR","stacomirtools",
 "purrr","janitor","patchwork","DBI","odbc","arrow","sf","sdmTMB")


if(length(setdiff(pkgs, rownames(installed.packages()))) > 0){
  install.packages(setdiff(pkgs, rownames(installed.packages())), dependencies = T)
}

invisible(lapply(pkgs, library, character.only = T))

# # Source code for map plots
devtools::source_url("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/VThunell/diasp-lht/main/R/functions/map-plot.R") 
Warning: `install_github()` was deprecated in devtools 2.5.0.
ℹ Please use pak::pak("user/repo") instead.
# source Eiriks conversion scripts and Durif stage scripts
source(file = "/Users/vitl0001/Documents/Projects/SWEel-lhts/script-functions-thawedsizes.R")
source(file = "/Users/vitl0001/Documents/Projects/SWEel-lhts/script-functions-durif.R")

options(ggplot2.continuous.colour = "viridis")
theme_set(theme_minimal()) 
# Set path
home <- here::here()

Get data

Sötebasen

Correct for thawing / ethanol Using E. Ryvoll-Åsheims functions based on Sundin et al. 2019. Do some renaming and calculate teh Durif index baed on Eirks Ryvoll-Åsheims script for that

# import data and convert 
sb_ind <- read.delim("/Users/vitl0001/Documents/Projects/SWEel-lhts/data/ÅlindividerSötebasen.csv", sep=";", dec=",", fileEncoding="Latin1") %>% convert_alsotebasen_thawed_sizes()
filter: removed 17 rows (<1%), 58,273 rows remaining
select: dropped 77 variables (InsamlingID, VattenNamn, RT90_X_Vatten, RT90_Y_Vatten, S99TM_N_Vatten, …)
pivot_longer: reorganized (Vikt1, Vikt2, Längd1, Längd2, KvalitetMätn1, …) into (Mätn, Vikt, Längd, KvalitetMätn) [was 58273x7, now 116546x5]
mutate: changed 25,067 values (22%) of 'Vikt' (0 new NAs)
        converted 'Längd' from integer to double (0 new NA)
        new variable 'priority' (double) with 6 unique values and 60% NA
summarise: now 58,273 rows and 3 columns, ungrouped
mutate: new variable 'VöD' (double) with 2,539 unique values and 56% NA
        new variable 'HöD' (double) with 2,510 unique values and 60% NA
        new variable 'VäD_corr' (double) with 3,861 unique values and 56% NA
        new variable 'HöD_corr' (double) with 3,712 unique values and 60% NA
        new variable 'HöBr_corr' (double) with 4,577 unique values and 64% NA
        new variable 'VäBr_corr' (double) with 4,886 unique values and 61% NA
left_join: added 2 columns (Vikt_corr, Längd_corr)
           > rows only in mutate(ålsötebasen, VöD..      17
           > rows only in d_corrected_masslength    (     0)
           > matched rows                            58,273
           >                                        ========
           > rows total                              58,290
colnames(sb_ind)
 [1] "InsamlingID"           "VattenNamn"            "RT90_X_Vatten"        
 [4] "RT90_Y_Vatten"         "S99TM_N_Vatten"        "S99TM_E_Vatten"       
 [7] "WGS84_N_Vatten"        "WGS84_E_Vatten"        "Plats"                
[10] "RT90_X_Plats"          "RT90_Y_Plats"          "S99TM_N_Plats"        
[13] "S99TM_E_Plats"         "WGS84_N_Plats"         "WGS84_E_Plats"        
[16] "Årtal1"                "Årtal2"                "InsamlMetod"          
[19] "Ansvarig"              "Utförare"              "Syfte"                
[22] "InlagtAv"              "AnmInsamling"          "IndividID"            
[25] "Art"                   "FångstDatum"           "FångstDatumOsäkerhet" 
[28] "FångstWGS84N"          "FångstWGS84E"          "FångstRT90X"          
[31] "FångstRT90Y"           "ProvDatum"             "Åldersprov"           
[34] "Provkod"               "IndividNr"             "KvalitetMätn1"        
[37] "Längd1"                "Vikt1"                 "KvalitetMätn2"        
[40] "Längd2"                "Vikt2"                 "Behandling"           
[43] "Skada"                 "NOtoliter"             "Mage"                 
[46] "Kön"                   "Stadium"               "Genprov"              
[49] "FångstPlats"           "UtsattDatum"           "MärkeNr"              
[52] "Märkning"              "Märke2Nr"              "Märkning2"            
[55] "OtoKemi"               "PlattaNr"              "AlizMärkn"            
[58] "SrBaMärkn"             "SrMärkn"               "TypOtoKemi"           
[61] "ÖvrigProvtagning"      "Neuromaster"           "HöH"                  
[64] "HöV"                   "VäH"                   "VäV"                  
[67] "HöBr"                  "VäBr"                  "NAnguillicola"        
[70] "FörekomstAnguillicola" "SignIndivid"           "AnmIndivid"           
[73] "Instrument"            "Preparering"           "Läsare"               
[76] "Analysdatum"           "Vävnad"                "Ålder"                
[79] "ÅlderDagar"            "Pluszon"               "ÅlderAlt"             
[82] "Tydlighet"             "SignÅlder"             "AnmÅlder"             
[85] "VöD"                   "HöD"                   "VäD_corr"             
[88] "HöD_corr"              "HöBr_corr"             "VäBr_corr"            
[91] "Vikt_corr"             "Längd_corr"           
sb_ind2 <- sb_ind %>%
  rename(length = Längd_corr,
         weight = Vikt_corr,
         age = Ålder,
         sex = Kön,
         visual_stage = Stadium
         ) %>%
  filter(!(is.na(length) & is.na(weight) & Åldersprov == "Nej")) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  # get the eye diameter and pectoral fin measurement and take the mean to make sure we use all indiviudlas that have a meassuremnt.
  mutate(diameter_eye = mean(c(VäD_corr,HöD_corr), na.rm = TRUE),
         length_pectoral = mean(c(HöBr_corr,VäBr_corr), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Fix coordinates. These uses "." as dec
  mutate(across(c(WGS84_E_Vatten, WGS84_N_Vatten, WGS84_N_Plats, WGS84_E_Plats), readr::parse_number),
         # when Plats coords are missing, we use the Vatten name. ~800 eels lacks coords but many of h
         lon = if_else(is.na(WGS84_E_Plats), WGS84_E_Vatten, WGS84_E_Plats),
         lat = if_else(is.na(WGS84_N_Plats), WGS84_N_Vatten, WGS84_N_Plats),
         db = "sb",
         year = if_else(Årtal1 == Årtal2, Årtal1, year(as_date(FångstDatum)))
         ) %>%
  # calculate Durif according to Eirik Ryvoll-Åsheims script
  mutate(durif_stage = stage_durif(length,weight,diameter_eye,length_pectoral,descr=F))
rename: renamed 5 variables (sex, visual_stage, age, weight, length)
filter: removed 9,616 rows (16%), 48,674 rows remaining
mutate: new variable 'diameter_eye' (double) with 17,702 unique values and 48% NA
        new variable 'length_pectoral' (double) with 8,217 unique values and 53% NA
ungroup: no grouping variables remain
mutate: converted 'WGS84_N_Vatten' from character to double (13086 new NA)
        converted 'WGS84_E_Vatten' from character to double (13086 new NA)
        converted 'WGS84_N_Plats' from character to double (10697 new NA)
        converted 'WGS84_E_Plats' from character to double (10697 new NA)
        new variable 'lon' (double) with 265 unique values and 2% NA
        new variable 'lat' (double) with 264 unique values and 2% NA
        new variable 'db' (character) with one unique value and 0% NA
        new variable 'year' (double) with 69 unique values and <1% NA
mutate: new variable 'durif_stage' (character) with 7 unique values and 53% NA

Durif vs Visual

# check how durif correspond to visual
sb_ind2 %>% summarise(n = n(), .by = c(durif_stage,visual_stage)) %>% arrange(-n)
summarise: now 34 rows and 3 columns, ungrouped
# A tibble: 34 × 3
   durif_stage visual_stage       n
   <chr>       <chr>          <int>
 1 <NA>        ""             14113
 2 <NA>        "Yngel"         8709
 3 FIV         "Blankål"       4498
 4 FIV         ""              3683
 5 FIII        ""              3581
 6 FIII        "Blankål"       3472
 7 <NA>        "Gulål"         1611
 8 FIII        "Halvblank ål"  1358
 9 FIV         "Halvblank ål"  1337
10 FII         "Gulål"         1275
# ℹ 24 more rows
# check densities of durif stages vs visual for females
# lump/condense stages
sb_ind2 %>%
  drop_na(durif_stage,visual_stage,sex) %>%
  filter(sex %in% c("f"),
         visual_stage %in% c("Blankål","Halvblank ål","Gulål")) %>%
  mutate(redDurif_stage = case_when(durif_stage %in% c("I","FII","MII") ~ "I, MII & FII",
                                    durif_stage %in% c("FIII") ~ "FIII",
                                    durif_stage %in% c("FIV","FV") ~ "FIV & FV",
                                    #durif_stage %in% c("I") & sex == "m" ~ "m<3",
                                    .default = durif_stage)) %>%
  #summarise(n = n(), .by = c(durif_stage,visual_stage)) %>% arrange(visual_stage) %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_density(aes(length, color = redDurif_stage), alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_density(aes(length, fill = visual_stage), color = NA, alpha = 0.2) 
drop_na: removed 25,874 rows (53%), 22,800 rows remaining
filter: removed 9,026 rows (40%), 13,774 rows remaining
mutate: new variable 'redDurif_stage' (character) with 3 unique values and 0% NA

KUL

# Connect to SLU-server:
# AQ_con <- dbConnect(odbc(),
#                     Driver = "ODBC Driver 18 for SQL Server",
#                     Server = "aquadw_prod.db.slu.se",
#                     USER = "vitl0001", # set you name
#                     Database = "AquaDW_prod",
#                     Trusted_Connection = "yes",
#                     Encrypt = "no",
#                     encoding = "utf-8")
#  
# # Extract the table with all individual eel data.
#  
# KUL <- dbGetQuery(AQ_con,"select * from AquaDW_prod.mart.KUL_INDIVID_NEW where Art = 'Ål'")
# saveRDS(KUL, file = paste0( "/Users/vitl0001/Documents/Projects/SWEel-lhts/data/KUL_",Sys.Date(),".RData"))
kul_ind <- readRDS("/Users/vitl0001/Documents/Projects/SWEel-lhts/data/KUL_2026-05-04.RData")

kul_ind2 <- kul_ind %>% 
  filter(!(is.na(Slutlängd_cm) & is.na(Totalvikt_gram) & is.na(Ålder))) %>%
  rename(length = Slutlängd_mm,
         weight = Totalvikt_gram,
         #  weight2 = Vikt2,
         age = Ålder,
         sex = Kön,
         visual_stage = Artbestämning,
         year = Fiskeår
         ) %>%
  mutate(lon = Bäst_longitud_decimal_grader,
         lat = Bäst_latitud_decimal_grader,
         db = "kul")
filter: removed 121 rows (<1%), 32,881 rows remaining
rename: renamed 6 variables (visual_stage, year, sex, length, weight, …)
mutate: new variable 'lon' (double) with 80 unique values and 0% NA
        new variable 'lat' (double) with 80 unique values and 0% NA
        new variable 'db' (character) with one unique value and 0% NA

Join data

# can there be dupes between the dbs?
dupes <- c(kul_ind2$Ind_id,sb_ind2$IndividID)[duplicated(c(kul_ind2$Ind_id,sb_ind2$IndividID))]
kul_ind2 %>%
  filter(Ind_id %in% dupes) %>% select(length,age,db) %>% bind_cols(sb_ind2 %>% filter(IndividID %in% dupes) %>% select(length,age,db)) # likely not

# join the sb and kul data
cols <- c("length","weight","age","lat","lon","sex","visual_stage","durif_stage","db","year")
df <- sb_ind2 %>% 
  select(all_of(cols), VattenNamn) %>% 
  bind_rows(kul_ind2 %>% 
            mutate(durif_stage = NA) %>%
            select(all_of(cols), Lokal)) %>%
  mutate(sex = ifelse(sex %in% c("f","Hona"), "female", 
                      if_else( sex %in% c("m","Hane"), "male", NA)),
         ind_id = row_number())
select: dropped 88 variables (InsamlingID, RT90_X_Vatten, RT90_Y_Vatten, S99TM_N_Vatten, S99TM_E_Vatten, …)
mutate: new variable 'durif_stage' (logical) with one unique value and 100% NA
select: dropped 105 variables (Art, Artbestämning_Sölabkod, Behörighet, Fiske, FISKE_ID, …)
mutate: changed 81,523 values (>99%) of 'sex' (32,159 new NAs)
        new variable 'ind_id' (integer) with 81,555 unique values and 0% NA

Retrieve spatial info

# make data spatial object
df_sf <- st_as_sf(df %>% drop_na(lat,lon), coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326,  remove = FALSE)
drop_na: removed 858 rows (1%), 80,697 rows remaining
re <- open_dataset("/Users/vitl0001/Documents/Projects/DIASPARA/Habitat_db/refeel/", format = "parquet")
ca <- open_dataset("/Users/vitl0001/Documents/Projects/DIASPARA/Habitat_db/catchments/", format = "parquet")

# refeel countries
co_sf <- re %>%
  collect() %>%
  filter(are_lev_code %in% "Country") %>%
  mutate(geometry = st_as_sfc(geom_polygon), crs = 4326) %>%
  st_as_sf(sf_column_name = "geometry", crs = 4326)
filter: removed 165,340 rows (>99%), 80 rows remaining
mutate: new variable 'geometry' (list) with 40 unique values and 0% NA
        new variable 'crs' (double) with one unique value and 0% NA
# join in country info and drop non SE data points
df_co <- df_sf %>%
  st_join(co_sf %>% select(are_code), join = st_nearest_feature) %>%
  rename(country = are_code) %>%
  filter(country == "SE")
select: dropped 9 variables (are_id, are_are_id, are_lev_code, are_wkg_code, are_ismarine, …)
although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_nearest_feature assumes that
they are planar

rename: renamed one variable (country)
filter: removed 1,387 rows (2%), 79,310 rows remaining
plot_map_Nord +
  geom_point(data = df_co %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% add_utm_columns(ll_names = c("lon", "lat"), utm_crs = 32633), aes(X*1000, Y*1000, color = country), size = 0.1)
Warning: `guide_colourbar()` needs continuous scales.
# coastal polygon and sea basin (Norra/Södra Östersjön, Bittenviken, Västerhavet)
ba_sf <- read_sf("/Users/vitl0001/Documents/Projects/SWEel-lhts/Havsomraden_2016/Havsomraden_2016.shp") %>%
  st_transform(my_shp, crs = 4326) %>%
  group_by(COMP_AUTH) %>%
  summarise() %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(sea_basin = COMP_AUTH) %>%
  # rename the NA polygon to get fix these later
  mutate(sea_basin = if_else(is.na(sea_basin), "FIX", sea_basin))
group_by: one grouping variable (COMP_AUTH)
although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_union assumes that they are
planar

summarise: now 6 rows and 2 columns, ungrouped
ungroup: no grouping variables remain
rename: renamed one variable (sea_basin)
mutate: changed one value (17%) of 'sea_basin' (one fewer NA)
# set marine those that intersects with the basins
df_coba <- df_co %>%
  st_join(ba_sf, join = st_intersects) %>%
  mutate(marine = if_else(is.na(sea_basin), "no", "yes")) %>%
  select(-sea_basin)
although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersects assumes that they
are planar
although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersects assumes that they
are planar
mutate: new variable 'marine' (character) with 2 unique values and 0% NAselect: dropped one variable (sea_basin)
# assign basin to all individual but exclude the FIX basin and use st_nearest_feature
df_coba2 <- df_coba %>%
  st_join(ba_sf %>% filter(!sea_basin == "FIX"), join = st_nearest_feature)
filter: removed one row (17%), 5 rows remaining
although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_nearest_feature assumes that
they are planar
# catchments  / HARO
ca_sf <- ca %>%
  collect() %>%
  mutate(geometry = st_as_sfc(shape, EWKB = TRUE, crs = 4326),
         main_catch = main_bas) %>%
  st_as_sf(sf_column_name = "geometry") %>%
  select(main_catch) %>%
  group_by(main_catch) %>%
  summarise() %>%
  ungroup()
mutate: new variable 'geometry' (list) with 90,240 unique values and 0% NA
        new variable 'main_catch' (double) with 6,385 unique values and 0% NA
select: dropped 298 variables (objectid, hybas_id, next_down, next_sink, main_bas, …)
group_by: one grouping variable (main_catch)
although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_union assumes that they are
planar

summarise: now 6,385 rows and 2 columns, ungrouped
ungroup: no grouping variables remain
plot_map_Nord +
  geom_sf(data = ca_sf)
# add in catchments to individuals NOT caught in marine areas, then we can use st_nearest_feature
df_cobaca_inland <- df_coba2 %>%
  filter(marine == "no") %>% 
  st_join(ca_sf, join = st_nearest_feature)
filter: removed 40,276 rows (51%), 39,034 rows remaining
although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_nearest_feature assumes that
they are planar
# join the inland and marine spatial units data
df_all <- df_cobaca_inland %>%
  mutate(spat.unit = as.character(main_catch)) %>%
  bind_rows(df_coba2 %>%
              filter(marine == "yes") %>%
              # For costal spatial spatial units 
              #mutate(spat.unit = as.character(sea_basin))) %>% 
              mutate(spat.unit = if_else(is.na(Lokal), VattenNamn, Lokal))) %>%
  as_tibble()
mutate: new variable 'spat.unit' (character) with 54 unique values and 0% NA
filter: removed 39,034 rows (49%), 40,276 rows remaining
mutate: new variable 'spat.unit' (character) with 33 unique values and 0% NA
ca_sf2 <- ca_sf %>%
  filter(main_catch %in% df_cobaca_inland$main_catch)
filter: removed 6,331 rows (99%), 54 rows remaining

Data points in spatial units, catchments and basins

plot_map_Nord +
  geom_sf(data = ca_sf2, aes(fill = factor(main_catch)), color = NA,  alpha = 0.3)  +
  geom_point(data = df_all %>%
               add_utm_columns(ll_names = c("lon", "lat"), utm_crs = 32633), aes(X*1000, Y*1000, color = factor(spat.unit)), show.legend = FALSE, size = 0.1, ) +
  labs(caption = "color is spatial unit")
Warning: `guide_colourbar()` needs continuous scales.
`guide_colourbar()` needs continuous scales.
plot_map_Nord +
  geom_point(data = df_all %>%
               add_utm_columns(ll_names = c("lon", "lat"), utm_crs = 32633), aes(X*1000, Y*1000, color = sea_basin), size = 0.1) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(title.position = "top"))

Save data

saveRDS(df_all %>% st_drop_geometry(), file = paste0(home, "/data/sweel_data_",Sys.Date(),".RData"))
